pi_items.source: setup_task.sh injects noise spikes and data gaps into the miniSEED data for GE.KWP, replacing the good waveform with synthetic noise while other stations (TOLI, GSI, SANI, BKB) remain clean or are copied from clean ones.
setup_task.sh generates synthetic data with 'data gaps' by zeroing out the data section (bytes 64 to 512) and 'noise spikes' by injecting random noise bytes at the end of every 3rd record.
setup_task.sh explicitly manages SDS directories and scrttv config (streams.codes) for TOLI, GSI, KWP, SANI, and BKB.
task.json and setup_task.sh mention scrttv as the visualization tool and scconfig as the tool to disable bindings.
Standard classification for this type of task.
The task explicitly requires diagnosing anomalous stations by inspecting waveforms and reconfiguring network parameters.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a synthetically generated anomaly injected into the miniSEED data for station GE.KWP. The setup scripts specifically zero out byte ranges to simulate data gaps and inject random bytes to simulate noise spikes into KWP's record, while explicitly verifying that the other stations (GE.TOLI, GE.GSI, GE.SANI, GE.BKB) maintain clean, non-anomalous data.
